title: Binary ops expansion — bs_skip, bs_get_float, UTF ops, bs_match
purpose: The binary matching infrastructure (B-023a/b/c) landed with 8 of 21 BinaryOp variants implemented across 6 match arms: BsInitWritable, BsCreateBin (construction), BsStartMatch3, BsStartMatch4 (match context setup), BsGetInteger2 (integer extraction), BsGetBinary2 (sub-binary extraction), BsMatchString (literal matching), and BsTestTail2 (end-of-binary check). The remaining 13 variants fall through to UnsupportedOpcode. These block any BEAM code that uses float binary patterns, UTF string matching, bit skipping, or the OTP 25+ compound bs_match instruction. Gleam's bit array patterns compile to several of these opcodes. binary.rs is currently 621 lines. Adding 13 handlers would push it far over the 500-line limit. This brief splits the binary operations into focused submodules.
task: Split binary.rs into focused submodules (mod.rs, construction.rs, matching.rs, utf.rs), then implement the 13 missing BinaryOp handlers: BsSkipBits2, BsTestUnit, BsGetFloat2, BsGetTail, BsGetUtf8, BsSkipUtf8, BsGetUtf16, BsSkipUtf16, BsGetUtf32, BsSkipUtf32, BsGetPosition, BsSetPosition, and the compound BsMatch instruction.

requirements.title: Binary module split
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL decompose binary.rs (621 lines) into focused submodules: binary/mod.rs (dispatch function binary_op(), re-exports, ~50 lines), binary/construction.rs (bs_init_or_create, bs_put_* handlers), binary/matching.rs (existing match handlers plus new handlers from R2-R5), and binary/utf.rs (UTF-8/16/32 get and skip handlers from R4).
acceptance: New directory: crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/binary/
binary/mod.rs — dispatch function binary_op(), re-exports (~50 lines)
binary/construction.rs — bs_init_or_create, bs_put_* handlers (existing construction code)
binary/matching.rs — existing match handlers: bs_start_match, bs_get_integer, bs_get_binary, bs_match_string, bs_test_tail + new handlers from R2-R5
binary/utf.rs — UTF-8/16/32 get and skip handlers (R4)
All existing tests move to appropriate submodules
All existing functionality preserved — no behavioral changes

requirements.title: Skip and test operations — BsSkipBits2, BsTestUnit
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters BsSkipBits2, THE SYSTEM SHALL advance the match context position by Size * Unit bits without reading, branching to Fail if insufficient bits remain. WHEN the interpreter encounters BsTestUnit, THE SYSTEM SHALL test that the remaining bits in the match context are divisible by Unit, branching to Fail if not.
acceptance: BsSkipBits2: advance the match context position by Size * Unit bits without reading. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Size, Unit, Flags]. If insufficient bits remain, branch to Fail.
BsTestUnit: test that the remaining bits in the match context are divisible by Unit. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Unit]. Branch to Fail if not.
Both modify (or inspect) the match context's bit position
Test: skip 16 bits in a 32-bit binary, verify position advanced
Test: skip past end of binary, verify branch to fail
Test: test unit 8 on 24-bit remainder succeeds
Test: test unit 8 on 25-bit remainder fails

requirements.title: Float extraction and tail operations — BsGetFloat2, BsGetTail
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters BsGetFloat2, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract an IEEE 754 float from the match context where Size * Unit must be 32 or 64 bits, creating a boxed float on the process heap. WHEN the interpreter encounters BsGetTail, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract the remaining bytes of the match context as a binary, creating a sub-binary or copy on the heap.
acceptance: BsGetFloat2: extract an IEEE 754 float from the match context. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Live, Size, Unit, Flags, Destination]. Size * Unit must be 32 or 64 bits. Creates a boxed float on the process heap.
BsGetTail: extract the remaining bytes of the match context as a binary. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Live, Destination]. Creates a sub-binary or copy on the heap.
Test: extract a 64-bit float from a binary containing 3.14 in IEEE 754
Test: extract a 32-bit float
Test: get_tail after matching first 4 bytes of an 8-byte binary yields 4-byte binary
Test: get_tail on empty remainder yields empty binary

requirements.title: UTF encoding operations — get and skip for UTF-8/16/32
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters a UTF get operation (BsGetUtf8, BsGetUtf16, BsGetUtf32), THE SYSTEM SHALL read one encoded codepoint from the match context and store it as an integer, branching to Fail if the encoding is invalid. WHEN the interpreter encounters a UTF skip operation (BsSkipUtf8, BsSkipUtf16, BsSkipUtf32), THE SYSTEM SHALL advance past one encoded codepoint without storing, branching to Fail if invalid. THE SYSTEM SHALL validate all codepoints by rejecting surrogates (0xD800..0xDFFF) and values greater than 0x10FFFF.
acceptance: BsGetUtf8: read one UTF-8 encoded codepoint from the match context, store as integer. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Live, Flags, Destination]. Branch to Fail if invalid UTF-8 at current position.
BsSkipUtf8: skip one UTF-8 encoded codepoint without storing. Branch to Fail if invalid.
BsGetUtf16: read one UTF-16 encoded codepoint (2 or 4 bytes depending on surrogate pairs). Flags include endianness (big/little/native).
BsSkipUtf16: skip one UTF-16 codepoint.
BsGetUtf32: read one UTF-32 codepoint (always 4 bytes). Validate codepoint range (0..0x10FFFF, excluding surrogates).
BsSkipUtf32: skip one UTF-32 codepoint with validation.
All UTF ops must validate codepoints: reject surrogates (0xD800..0xDFFF), values > 0x10FFFF
Test: UTF-8 decode of ASCII (1 byte), 2-byte, 3-byte, and 4-byte sequences
Test: UTF-8 invalid sequence branches to fail
Test: UTF-16 big-endian and little-endian decode
Test: UTF-32 with valid and invalid codepoints

requirements.title: Match context navigation — BsGetPosition, BsSetPosition
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters BsGetPosition, THE SYSTEM SHALL read the match context's current bit offset as an integer term and store it in the destination register. WHEN the interpreter encounters BsSetPosition, THE SYSTEM SHALL set the match context's current bit offset from an integer term in the source register.
acceptance: BsGetPosition: read the match context's current bit offset as an integer term and store in the destination register. Operands: [MatchContext, Destination, Live].
BsSetPosition: set the match context's current bit offset from an integer term in the source register. Operands: [MatchContext, Source].
These support the compiler's backtracking optimization for complex binary patterns
Test: start match, get position (should be 0), skip 8 bits, get position (should be 8), set position to 0, get integer should re-read first byte

requirements.title: Compound binary match — BsMatch
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter encounters BsMatch (opcode 182), THE SYSTEM SHALL process a fail label, a match context, and a list of match commands encoded as operands, executing each sub-command sequentially. WHEN any sub-command fails, THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to Fail with the match context position unchanged (entire match is atomic).
acceptance: BsMatch: takes a fail label, a match context, and a list of match commands encoded as operands. Operands: [Fail, MatchContext, Commands...] where Commands is a nested operand list.

Each command type maps to an equivalent individual operation: {ensure, Live, Bits} — check remaining bits; {integer, Live, Flags, Size, Unit, Dst} — like BsGetInteger2; {float, Live, Flags, Size, Unit, Dst} — like BsGetFloat2; {binary, Live, Flags, Size, Unit, Dst} — like BsGetBinary2; {skip, Stride} — like BsSkipBits2; {get_tail, Live, Dst} — like BsGetTail; {'=:=', Live, Bits, Value} — match exact value
If any sub-command fails, branch to Fail (entire match is atomic — match context position is not partially advanced on failure)

The operand encoding follows the BEAM extended operand format — reference OTP source lib/compiler/src/beam_disasm.erl and erts/emulator/beam/jit/beam_jit_common.cpp (bs_match handler) for the encoding spec rather than reverse-engineering from compiled .beam files
Test: compound match extracting integer + skip + binary in sequence
Test: compound match with failing sub-command branches to fail, position unchanged

boundaries: SHALL implement the 13 missing BinaryOp handlers
SHALL NOT modify the existing 8 implemented handlers across 6 match arms (they work correctly)
SHALL NOT add new binary term types — all operations work on the existing MatchContext and binary heap representations from B-023a
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new binary operation tests
All 13 previously-unsupported BinaryOp variants no longer return UnsupportedOpcode
binary.rs replaced by binary/ directory with mod.rs, construction.rs, matching.rs, utf.rs
Existing 8 binary op handlers produce identical results after the split
UTF operations correctly validate codepoints and reject surrogates
BsMatch compound instruction handles all sub-command types atomically
